feedback.text: In my opinion, the Digital Fairness Act is a great thing and I welcome more protection as a consumer. Would it also be possible for cosumers to be protected if their purchased games, more specifically their digital video games, are destroyed by the publisher? You should also be allowed to own your games digitally, or at least get their money back, unless otherwise. Otherwise, you simply lose the game, and thus his money, and have nothing to do. The Stop Killing Games Initiative is already committed to this and has enough signatures and support from all over the world. It would be an appropriate complement to the Digital Fairness Act. I thank you for your attention.
